programs.name: Nevada's Senior RX Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides subsidies to help seniors cover prescription medication costs, including assistance with Medicare Part D expenses for eligible members and cost-sharing for those not eligible. Offers additional services like the Diabetic Sense program for medication compliance and vision benefits for qualifying members.
programs.who_its_for: Nevada residents aged 62 or older with household income not exceeding $25,477 (single) or $33,963 (married couples), who must enroll in an approved Medicare Part D plan if eligible.
programs.whats_unique: Nevada's state-funded pharmaceutical assistance program offering up to $5,100 annual subsidy, distinct from federal programs like Medicare Part D Low-Income Subsidy.

programs.name: Home and Community-Based Waiver for the Frail Elderly (HCBW-FE)
programs.what_it_does: Offers non-medical supportive services to help seniors avoid nursing home placement and stay in their homes or assisted living facilities. Covers services based on level of care needs through limited slots.
programs.who_its_for: Nevada residents aged 65 or older at risk of nursing home placement, meeting financial and medical eligibility criteria.
programs.whats_unique: Nevada-specific Medicaid waiver with limited enrollment (waitlists possible), providing home- and community-based alternatives beyond standard federal Medicaid.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by services and level of care; non-entitlement with waitlists[3]

programs.name: Community Options Program for the Elderly (COPE)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers non-medical services to support independence at home as an alternative to nursing home care. Assesses eligibility based on risk of institutionalization.
programs.who_its_for: Nevada residents aged 65 or older at risk of nursing home placement, meeting financial guidelines and nursing home level of care.
programs.whats_unique: Nevada state program similar to but separate from HCBW-FE waiver, funded to provide home-based services without Medicaid waiver constraints.
programs.benefit_value: Non-medical services tailored to needs; call for current income guidelines[5]

programs.name: Social Security Domiciliary Rate
programs.what_it_does: Increases SSI payments for seniors moving to residential facilities or assisted living when needing help with daily activities. Requires facility admission documents for application.
programs.who_its_for: Nevada SSI recipients aged 65+ with income below the domiciliary rate, residing in RFG or ALF.
programs.whats_unique: Nevada-specific SSI supplement to cover domiciliary care costs in facilities, applied through local Social Security offices.
programs.benefit_value: SSI increase to domiciliary rate based on facility level[2]

programs.name: Taxi Assistance Program (TAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides subsidized taxi transportation services for seniors. Supports mobility needs unique to Nevada's urban and rural areas.
programs.who_its_for: Nevada seniors; specific eligibility through Aging and Disability Services Division.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded transportation program listed among Nevada-specific senior services, not a standard federal offering.
programs.benefit_value: Subsidized taxi rides; details via ADSD[8]
